rationale: In the citing paper, Zhao, Hoeffler, and Zauberman (2007) appears once in a grouped citation that lists prior work showing differences between process- and outcome-focused simulation across several outcomes. It is used as background support for the general claim that simulation focus can matter, specifically for preference consistency over time. The citing paper does not give this study a distinct role in hypothesis derivation, method choice, or interpretation beyond that brief literature listing. Therefore, this is best classified as a passing mention rather than a substantive citation relation.
